title: Veronica pasquorum
intro: Veronica pasquorum, also known as Corn Speedwell, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Veronicaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Veronica pasquorum include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Veronica pasquorum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to relieve coughs, congestion, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Veronica pasquorum belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Veronica. Veronica pasquorum is native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of this plant is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Lamiales, family - Plantaginaceae, genus - Veronica, species - V. pasquorum. This classification is based on its morphology and genetic characteristics.
